title: Document Python TUI support matrix and smoke-test procedure
description: Why this exists: the client spans multiple transports and host OS Bluetooth stacks, so supported combinations must be explicit. What needs to be done: document supported host OS versions, BLE stack assumptions, IP/CoAP path assumptions, firmware prerequisites, known unsupported operations, visual target notes, and manual smoke-test procedures for both direct BLE and IP/CoAP. Acceptance criteria: docs cover Linux BLE, macOS BLE, IP/CoAP local node path, fake/native_sim mode, and hardware smoke test; limitations are explicit; missing hardware validation creates follow-up Beads rather than being hidden.
notes:
Implementation evidence for Python native TUI support matrix and smoke procedure:
- Added docs/python-native-tui-support.md with command matrix, host support matrix, firmware LCI prerequisites, known limitations, no-hardware smoke test, IP/CoAP manual smoke test, and BLE manual smoke test gated on the BLE ResourceTransport bridge.
- Linked the support matrix from python/README.md.
- Documented scoped link-local CoAP URI examples using %25 zone escaping and advised ULA/GUA addresses when available.
- Created follow-up project-LICHEN-mg7z for physical Python native TUI validation against real LCI firmware over IP/CoAP and BLE when available.

Local validation:
- git diff --check -> passed.
- cd python && uv run pytest tests/test_packaging.py -q -> 3 passed in 0.11s.

Code review:
- Bernoulli review: no blocking P0/P1/P2 findings; support matrix separates fake/native paths from live IP/CoAP and blocked BLE, limitations explicit, physical validation tracked by project-LICHEN-mg7z.
- Fermat final re-review: no P0/P1/P2 findings; scoped link-local examples and uv-run smoke command address earlier findings, docs avoid overclaiming BLE support.

No EC2 used for this documentation-only task.
